expectations.text: Output uses Given/When/Then format with AC-N numbering
expectations.evidence: All 14 criteria use bold AC-N numbering (AC-1 through AC-14) with Given/When/Then format, organized under coverage categories.

expectations.text: Includes AC for normal request under limit (happy path)
expectations.evidence: AC-2: 'Given a standard API key that has made fewer than 100 requests... Then the request is processed normally with a 2xx response'

expectations.text: Includes AC for request when limit is exceeded (429 response)
expectations.evidence: AC-5: 'Given a standard API key that has made 100 requests... Then the response status is 429 Too Many Requests'

expectations.text: Includes AC for Retry-After header correctness
expectations.evidence: AC-10: 'Given a standard API key that has exhausted its rate limit... Then waiting N seconds and retrying results in a successful request'

expectations.text: Includes AC for premium user higher limit (1000/min)
expectations.evidence: AC-3 and AC-4 cover premium user requests with 1000 limit. AC-6 covers premium limit exceeded.

expectations.text: Includes AC for rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset)
expectations.evidence: AC-1 explicitly lists all three headers with descriptions. AC-12 specifically covers X-RateLimit-Reset semantics.

expectations.text: Includes AC for edge case: sliding window reset behavior
expectations.evidence: AC-8: 'Given a standard API key that hit 100 requests... When the oldest request ages past 60 seconds... Then subsequent requests are allowed again'

expectations.text: Includes AC for missing or invalid API key
expectations.evidence: AC-7: 'Given a request with an invalid or missing API key... Then the request is rejected with the appropriate authentication error (401/403)'

expectations.text: At least 8 distinct acceptance criteria are defined
expectations.evidence: 14 distinct acceptance criteria defined (AC-1 through AC-14).
